evals.expected_output: Should flag the silent failure: returning null instead of throwing removes auth enforcement. Callers expecting a User object will get null. This is a Tier 1 correctness/security issue (auth loosening). Should NOT just say 'looks good' because the description says 'graceful handling'.
expectations: Identifies returning null as a security or correctness issue, not just a style preference
Explains that callers expecting a User object will receive null, causing downstream failures or auth bypass
Classifies this as a must-fix or blocking issue, not a suggestion
Does not accept the change just because the PR description frames it as graceful handling

evals.expected_output: Should catch the incomplete refactor: src/api/posts.ts still imports formatDate (old name) but the export was renamed to formatTimestamp. This will break at runtime. Should classify as Tier 1 breakage.
expectations: Identifies that src/api/posts.ts still imports formatDate which no longer exists
Explains this will cause a runtime or compile error
Classifies this as a breakage or must-fix issue
Does not get distracted by the files that were correctly updated

evals.expected_output: Should flag the silent failure in the catch block: payment errors are swallowed with no logging, no error details returned, and no way for callers to know what went wrong. This is Tier 2 error handling. Should also note that the customer could be charged but the DB update could fail (partial state), and the catch swallows that too.
expectations: Identifies the catch block as swallowing errors silently
Notes that no error details are logged or returned to callers
Flags the risk of partial state: charge succeeds but DB update fails, caught and hidden
Does not accept this as adequate error handling just because the PR says it adds error handling

evals.prompt:
Review this PR. Here's the diff:

```diff
--- a/src/config.ts
+++ b/src/config.ts
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 export const config = {
   apiUrl: process.env.API_URL || 'http://localhost:3000',
-  dbHost: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
+  dbHost: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
   logLevel: process.env.LOG_LEVEL || 'info',
+  stripeKey: 'sk_live_abc123xyz789',
 };
```
evals.expected_output: Should immediately flag the hardcoded Stripe live secret key as a Tier 1 security issue. Should recommend using an environment variable. Should NOT flag the unchanged dbHost line or the existing pattern of env vars — those are pre-existing.
expectations: Flags the hardcoded Stripe secret key as a security issue
Classifies it as a critical or must-fix finding
Recommends using an environment variable instead
Does not flag the unchanged lines or pre-existing patterns as issues

evals.prompt:
Review this PR: ```diff --- a/src/components/UserList.tsx +++ b/src/components/UserList.tsx @@ -5,10 +5,10 @@ function UserList({ users }: Props) { return (
- {users.map(user => ( -
- {user.name} + {users.map((user) => ( +
+ {user.name} {user.email}
))} ``` PR title: 'Fix linting warnings in UserList component'
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is purely formatting/style changes (arrow function parens, whitespace). Should approve with no issues — this is exactly the kind of thing the skill says NOT to flag (style and formatting, linters handle this).
expectations: Recognizes the changes are purely stylistic or formatting
Does not flag any issues
Approves or states no issues found
Does not invent findings to justify the review

evals.prompt: I just pushed some changes to my feature branch but haven't created a PR yet. Can you review what I've changed before I open the PR?
evals.expected_output: Should handle self-review mode: use local git to diff against base branch (git merge-base HEAD main), review locally, and report findings directly instead of posting API comments. Should not require a PR number or try to call any platform API.
expectations: Uses local git commands to get the diff (git diff, git merge-base, not API calls)
Does not ask for or require a PR number
Reports findings directly to the user instead of posting comments via API
Still applies the same review criteria (tiers, philosophy) to the local diff

evals.expected_output: Should flag multiple issues: no error handling for partial deletion (if orders delete but payments fail, data is inconsistent — should use a transaction), no null check on user (findById could return null, then user.email throws), and no test coverage for a destructive operation. Should NOT flag naming or style.
expectations: Identifies the lack of transaction or atomicity for cascading deletes
Identifies that user could be null if findById returns nothing, causing user.email to throw
Flags missing error handling for the multi-step deletion sequence
Does not flag naming conventions or style issues

evals.expected_output: Should flag the SQL injection vulnerability as Tier 1 must-fix. Should show the review to the user first (per skill step 7), then describe the posting commands. Every posted comment body must include the attribution footer mentioning 'Claude Code' and the user's name (arthur-dev). The footer is mandatory — must NOT be omitted, paraphrased, or hidden.
expectations: Identifies the SQL injection vulnerability from string interpolation in the query
Classifies the SQL injection as Tier 1 / must-fix / critical security issue
Shows the review to the user before describing API posting commands
Includes an attribution footer in the posted comment body that mentions 'Claude Code'
The attribution footer references the approving user 'arthur-dev'
Does not omit or paraphrase the attribution footer
